items.a: The course is fully in-person at the Santa Cruz Pharma Care lab in Bayamón. Three consecutive days, six hours each (18 hours total), with theory and supervised practice on the same day. Each day is a module: regulation + capsules; suppositories, oral liquids, and dermatology; topical hormones and special cases.

Yes. Upon completing every module and passing both the practical and written assessments, you receive a Santa Cruz Compounding Academy professional certificate documenting instruction hours and competencies in USP 〈795〉 and USP 〈800〉. It serves as evidence of continuing education and of audit readiness.

You need an active pharmacist license or status as a pharmacy technician/student in pharmacy or pharmacy technology. The course covers USP 〈795〉 and 〈800〉 in a single cohort; there are no modular prerequisites. If you're unsure, write to us before enrolling.

items.q: How do I access the course material?
items.a: Once your enrollment is confirmed you receive access to the student portal with the theoretical material, cohort calendar, USP reference reading, and downloadables. The hands-on sessions are scheduled in blocks at the Bayamón campus and announced at least two weeks in advance.

items.q: Do you have plans for pharmacy teams?
items.a: Yes. For pharmacies that want to train their full team we offer private cohorts — either at our Bayamón campus or, depending on scope, at the client's site. Includes curriculum personalization and a per-participant competency report. Write to us to coordinate.

Yes. A 100 % refund if you request 30 or more calendar days before the cohort starts; 50 % if you request between 14 and 30 days before; no monetary refund with fewer than 14 days' notice or once it has begun. At any time before the first day you can also transfer your seat — to another person or to a future cohort — at no penalty, preserving the full value. Details on the Refund Policy page.

privacy.title: Privacy Policy
privacy.intro: Santa Cruz Compounding Academy, LLC ("SCCA", "we") respects your privacy and is committed to protecting the personal data you entrust to us. This Privacy Policy describes what information we collect when you visit or interact with sccompoundingacademy.com (the "Site"), how we use it, with whom we share it, and what your rights are. It applies to all individuals who visit the Site, request information, or enroll in any of our educational programs.

sections.heading: 1. Information we collect
sections.body:
We collect the information you yourself provide when you complete forms on the Site, enroll in a course, or contact us. This includes, as applicable: full name, email address, phone number, professional license number (when applicable), payment information (processed by our payment processor — see section 4), and any comments or inquiries you send us. For students requesting the reduced rate, we also collect a photo of the student ID (matrícula) before payment. Because it is identity documentation, it receives heightened handling: it is stored temporarily on Vercel Blob, reviewed only by authorized SCCA staff (including via a secure link sent by email) to verify eligibility, and permanently deleted once a decision is made, whether approval or rejection. The lawful basis is performance of the educational contract at the reduced rate.

We also automatically collect certain technical information when you browse the Site: IP address, browser type and version, operating system, pages visited, date and duration of the visit, and the referrer (URL you came from). This information is collected through cookies and similar technologies.

We do not collect special categories of sensitive data (racial origin, political opinions, health, sexual orientation, etc.) unless you expressly provide them for an identified legitimate purpose.

sections.heading: 2. How we use your information
sections.body:
We use your personal information for the following purposes: (a) processing your enrollment and charging the course fee; (b) communicating with you about your enrollment, cohort schedule, program changes, and related matters; (c) issuing completion certificates and assessment reports; (d) responding to your inquiries and support requests; (e) complying with applicable legal, tax, and regulatory obligations in Puerto Rico; (f) preventing fraud, abuse, or misuse of the Site; and (g) improving our services through aggregated and anonymized usage analysis.

We do not use your information for third-party advertising nor do we sell it to marketing entities.

sections.heading: 3. Why we process your data
sections.body:
We process your personal data only when there is a concrete operational or legal justification: (a) to perform the educational contract when you enroll in a course; (b) to comply with applicable legal, tax, and regulatory obligations in Puerto Rico, including requirements of the PR Department of Treasury and of the Puerto Rico Board of Pharmacy when applicable; (c) to operate and improve our services responsibly; and (d) with your express consent when we request it for specific purposes (for example, optional subscription to non-transactional communications).

SCCA does not transfer or sell your information to third parties for marketing or advertising purposes.

sections.heading: 4. Service providers (processors)
sections.body:
We share limited data with trusted third-party providers, each under contractual obligation to protect your information:

Stripe, Inc. — processes card payments. We do not store card numbers on our servers; Stripe receives them directly under PCI-DSS compliance. Policy: https://stripe.com/privacy

Resend — delivers transactional emails (enrollment confirmation, cohort reminders, operational communications). Policy: https://resend.com/legal/privacy-policy

Airtable — stores the enrollment registry, cohorts, and assessment results. Policy: https://www.airtable.com/company/privacy

Vercel, Inc. — hosts the Site and maintains technical access logs. Policy: https://vercel.com/legal/privacy-policy

Google Maps — campus map embed; when the page loads, your browser may send your IP to Google. Policy: https://policies.google.com/privacy

Neon, Inc. — managed database where enrollment records and user accounts are stored. Policy: https://neon.tech/privacy-policy

Vercel Blob — temporary storage of the matrícula photo during verification; deleted once the decision is made. Policy: https://vercel.com/legal/privacy-policy

Some of these providers process data outside Puerto Rico (primarily in the continental United States). By using the Site, you accept this transfer.

sections.heading: 5. Cookies, server logs, and similar technologies
sections.body:
We distinguish between two categories of technical data:

Browser cookies: the Site uses only cookies strictly necessary for its operation (language preference, session, and basic security). These do not require prior consent under applicable regulations and are not used for advertising or cross-site tracking.

Server logs: our infrastructure providers (Vercel) and our own backend record technical information about each HTTP request, including IP address, User-Agent, requested URL, response code, and timestamp. These logs are used exclusively for operational diagnostics, security, and fraud prevention.

If we incorporate aggregated analytics tools in the future (for example, Plausible Analytics), we will update this Policy and enable the consent mechanisms that may apply. You can configure your browser to reject cookies, although this may affect Site functionality.

SCCA does not currently respond to browser 'Do Not Track' signals because we do not perform the advertising or cross-site tracking those signals are intended to limit.

sections.heading: 6. Retention period
sections.body:
We retain your personal information only for as long as necessary to fulfill the purposes described in this policy, except where a legal or tax obligation requires longer periods. As a general reference:

Enrollment and payment data: up to seven (7) years, per PR Department of Treasury requirements for accounting records.

Inquiry emails without subsequent enrollment: up to two (2) years from last contact.

Issued assessment results and certificates: up to ten (10) years from certificate issuance, a reasonable period for later professional verification. After that period, records are anonymized or deleted unless the data subject requests otherwise.

Server logs and technical browsing data: up to twelve (12) months.

Student ID (matrícula) photo: permanently deleted once verification is complete (whether approval or rejection); not retained.

Once these periods expire, data is irreversibly deleted or anonymized, except for additional retention required by court order, regulatory requirement, or active litigation.

sections.heading: 7. Your rights
sections.body:
You have the following rights regarding your personal information: (a) access — request a copy of the data we hold about you; (b) rectification — correct inaccurate or incomplete information; (c) deletion — request erasure, except where law or active litigation requires retention; (d) limitation — ask us to restrict processing in specific circumstances; (e) portability — receive your data in a structured, readable format; (f) objection — object to processing based on operational interests.

To exercise any of these rights, write to us at info@sccompoundingacademy.com clearly identifying your request and your identity. We respond within a maximum of thirty (30) calendar days. We do not charge for these requests unless they are manifestly unfounded or excessively repetitive.

California residents: if you are a resident of the State of California, you may have additional rights under the California Consumer Privacy Act (CCPA), including the right to know what information we collect about you and the right to opt out of the sale of data. SCCA does not sell personal information within the meaning of the CCPA. To exercise CCPA rights, write to the same address.

Complaints: if you believe SCCA's processing of your data infringes applicable legislation, you may file a complaint with the Puerto Rico Department of Consumer Affairs (DACO).

sections.heading: 8. Security
sections.body:
We implement reasonable technical and organizational measures to protect your information: TLS encryption in transit, role-based access control to our systems, and selection of providers with recognized security standards (PCI-DSS for payment processing, SOC 2 Type II for infrastructure and storage). However, no system on the internet is 100 % secure; if you detect or suspect a security incident, report it immediately to info@sccompoundingacademy.com.

Incident notification (Puerto Rico Act 111-2005): in the event of a security breach that compromises personal information of Puerto Rico residents, we will notify those affected and the Department of Consumer Affairs (DACO) within the timeframes and in the manner established by Act No. 111 of September 7, 2005 ('Act for the Protection of Personal Information in Information Databases'), including the nature of the incident, the types of data involved, and the corrective measures adopted or recommended.

sections.heading: 9. Minors
sections.body: The Site and SCCA's educational programs are directed exclusively at persons eighteen (18) years of age or older. We do not accept enrollments from, nor intentionally collect personal information from, minors. If we discover we have received information from a minor, we will delete it without delay and cancel any associated enrollment. If you have reason to believe that a minor has provided personal data to us, contact us at info@sccompoundingacademy.com.

sections.heading: 10. International transfers
sections.body:
As noted in Section 4, some of our providers operate servers outside Puerto Rico, primarily in the continental United States. Each of these providers is subject to contractual security obligations and maintains industry certifications (PCI-DSS for payment processing, SOC 2 Type II for infrastructure). By using the Site you acknowledge and accept these transfers.

SCCA does not operate its own infrastructure outside Puerto Rico nor transfer data to jurisdictions other than those used by these providers. If in the future residents of the European Union or of jurisdictions with specific international transfer requirements access the Site, we will evaluate and implement the necessary contractual mechanisms (for example, Standard Contractual Clauses) before processing their data.

sections.heading: 11. Changes to this policy
sections.body: We may update this Privacy Policy when we add new services or operational changes. Any material change will be notified via a visible notice on the Site and, when applicable, by email to active enrollees. The "Last updated" date at the start of the document reflects the version in force.

sections.heading: 12. Contact
sections.body:
For questions, requests, or complaints about this Privacy Policy or the treatment of your personal data:

Santa Cruz Compounding Academy, LLC
73 Santa Cruz Medical Building, Suite 201
Bayamón, Puerto Rico 00961
Email: info@sccompoundingacademy.com
Phone (primary): 787-408-5775
Phone (secondary): 787-798-4646

terms.title: Terms and Conditions
terms.intro: These Terms and Conditions ("Terms") govern access to sccompoundingacademy.com and enrollment in the educational programs offered by Santa Cruz Compounding Academy, LLC ("SCCA", "we"). By enrolling in any course or using the Site, you accept these Terms in full. If you do not agree, refrain from enrolling or using the Site.

sections.heading: 1. Nature of the service
sections.body:
SCCA offers in-person professional training programs in pharmaceutical compounding, aligned with USP chapters 〈795〉 (non-sterile preparations) and 〈800〉 (hazardous drug handling). Courses are delivered at our campus in Bayamón, Puerto Rico, in cohort format with limited seats.

Certificates issued by SCCA document instruction hours and acquired competencies. They do not, by themselves, constitute official continuing education (CE) accreditation unless we expressly indicate so in writing at the start of a specific cohort.

sections.heading: 2. Eligibility
sections.body:
To enroll you must be over 18 years of age and have legal capacity to enter into contracts. Programs are directed at licensed pharmacists, pharmacy technicians, pharmacy owners or administrators, and advanced students in pharmacy or pharmacy technology. Some programs may require specific technical prerequisites indicated in each course's description.

SCCA reserves the right to validate information provided at enrollment and to reject or cancel enrollments when false, incomplete, or unverifiable data is detected.

sections.heading: 3. Enrollment and payment process
sections.body:
Enrollment is completed through the form available on the Site. Payment is processed securely through Stripe at the time of enrollment. We accept Visa, Mastercard, American Express, and Discover credit and debit cards. Charges are made in U.S. dollars (USD).

An enrollment is considered confirmed only when payment has been completed successfully and the student has received the confirmation email generated automatically by the system. If payment fails for any reason, the seat is not reserved.

Published prices are expressed in USD and, unless expressly stated otherwise at checkout, do not include the Puerto Rico Sales and Use Tax (IVU) or equivalent taxes that may apply. The final breakdown, including any applicable taxes, is shown on the checkout screen before payment confirmation.

Issuing-bank fees: any additional charge applied by the card-issuing bank (for example, currency conversion fees, international transaction fees, or intermediation charges) is the sole responsibility of the student and is not refundable by SCCA.

sections.heading: 4. Refund policy
sections.body: Our complete refund policy is available on the Refund Policy page. In summary: with thirty (30) or more calendar days' notice before the cohort starts, a one hundred percent (100 %) refund; between fourteen (14) and thirty (30) days, a fifty percent (50 %) refund; with fewer than fourteen (14) days or once the cohort has begun, no monetary refund is granted. In all cases, before the first day you may transfer your seat — to another person or to a future cohort — at no penalty, preserving the full value (see Refund Policy).

sections.heading: 5. Student obligations
sections.body:
By enrolling you agree to: (a) attend in-person sessions according to the cohort's published calendar; (b) comply with laboratory safety standards, including correct use of provided or required personal protective equipment under USP 〈800〉; (c) not photograph, record, or share instructional material, procedures, or work of other students without express written authorization from SCCA and from the persons involved; (d) behave respectfully toward instructors, staff, and cohort peers; (e) refrain from any conduct that puts at risk your own or others' physical integrity, the facilities, or the material; (f) provide truthful information and keep it updated throughout the cohort; and (g) authorize SCCA to take photographs or video of the cohort environment for educational, documentary, or institutional marketing purposes, on the understanding that (i) SCCA will make reasonable efforts to anonymize identifiable imagery upon student request, and (ii) you may request exclusion in writing before the cohort or at its start by emailing info@sccompoundingacademy.com.

Failure to meet obligations (a) through (f) may result in expulsion from the cohort without right to refund, at SCCA's reasonable discretion and with prior notice when practicable.

sections.heading: 6. Intellectual property
sections.body:
All instructional material — manuals, presentations, videos, templates, procedure sheets, exercises, and online portal materials — is the exclusive property of SCCA, its instructors, or its licensors, and is protected by the intellectual property laws of Puerto Rico and the United States.

You are granted a personal, non-exclusive, non-transferable, revocable license to use the material exclusively for your own learning during and after the cohort. It is expressly prohibited to reproduce, distribute, publish, modify, sell, or create derivative works from the material without prior written authorization from SCCA.

Access to the online portal and its materials is personal and non-transferable. Each enrollment grants a single individual access license. You may not download, screen-capture, circumvent the technical protection measures, or redistribute the portal materials, nor share your access credentials with third parties. Non-compliance may result in immediate revocation of access without a refund, without prejudice to other actions.

The names "Santa Cruz Compounding Academy" and "SCCA" and the logo (chartreuse shield with mortar) are trademarks of SCCA. They may not be used, reproduced, or associated with third-party products or services without prior written authorization from SCCA. The registration status of these marks with the Puerto Rico Department of State or with the USPTO is available upon request.

sections.heading: 7. References to USP
sections.body: References to USP chapters 〈795〉 and 〈800〉 in our materials and communications are made for educational and regulatory-compliance purposes. "USP" and the chapter numbers are property of the United States Pharmacopeial Convention, Inc. SCCA is not affiliated with, sponsored by, or endorsed by USP, and our certificates are not official USP certificates.

sections.heading: 8. Certification
sections.body:
Upon completing all sessions of a program and passing the required assessments, you will receive a certificate of completion issued by SCCA. The certificate documents: (a) your name, (b) the completed program, (c) hours of in-person instruction, (d) competencies assessed, and (e) date of issuance.

Sponsored CE accreditation: Specific SCCA cohorts are offered as accredited Continuing Education activities under the sponsorship of the Colegio de Farmacéuticos de Puerto Rico (ACPE Provider 0151). When a cohort is offered under this sponsorship, the cohort description and the issued certificate state so expressly, including the recognized contact hours and CEUs (18 contact hours / 1.8 CEUs for the current curriculum).

SCCA, as an independent entity, is not an accredited provider with ACPE nor with the Puerto Rico Board of Pharmacy. CE recognition of any given cohort comes from the sponsoring provider (Colegio de Farmacéuticos PR), not from SCCA. For cohorts without explicit sponsorship, the certificate documents participation, but its acceptance as accredited CE hours depends on the student's employer, regulatory board, or receiving institution.

sections.heading: 9. Limitation of liability
sections.body:
Educational content is provided for professional training purposes. SCCA does not guarantee specific professional results — including, without limitation, passing regulatory exams, obtaining employment, third-party CE recognition, operational compliance of a specific pharmacy, or clinical outcomes derived from preparations the student formulates after the course. The student is responsible for applying the acquired knowledge according to their professional judgment and the regulations applicable in their jurisdiction.

To the maximum extent permitted by law, SCCA's total liability to the student — for any claim arising from or related to these Terms, the delivery of the course, or use of the Site — is limited to the amount actually paid by the student for the course giving rise to the claim. SCCA will not be liable for indirect, incidental, special, punitive, or consequential damages, nor for lost profits, even if it has been advised of the possibility of such damages.

Nothing in this clause limits liabilities that under the Puerto Rico Civil Code (Act No. 55 of June 1, 2020, as amended) or any other applicable law cannot be contractually excluded, including willful misconduct, gross negligence, or physical injuries caused directly by SCCA or its instructors.

sections.heading: 10. Program modifications
sections.body: SCCA reserves the right to modify dates, schedules, instructors, location, or specific content of a cohort for operational reasons, force majeure, or pedagogical improvement. When a change is substantial, enrolled students will be notified with as much advance notice as possible and, when the rescheduling is not acceptable to the student, a full refund or transfer to a future cohort will be offered.

sections.heading: 11. Modifications to these Terms
sections.body: We may update these Terms when we add new services, operational changes, or due to legal requirements. The current version will be published on the Site with the update date at the start. Material changes will be notified by email to active students. Continued use of the Site or enrollment in cohorts after a change constitutes acceptance of the updated Terms.

sections.heading: 12. Dispute resolution and governing law
sections.body:
These Terms are governed by the laws of the Commonwealth of Puerto Rico, without regard to its conflict-of-laws principles, and by applicable U.S. federal law. Any dispute arising from or relating to these Terms shall be submitted to the exclusive jurisdiction of the competent courts of the Judicial District of Bayamón, Puerto Rico, or of the federal courts with jurisdiction over the District of Puerto Rico when applicable.

Before initiating any formal proceeding, the parties agree to attempt to resolve disputes in good faith through direct communication for a period of no less than thirty (30) calendar days from the first written notice of the dispute. For eligible claims, the consumer may additionally seek recourse with the Puerto Rico Department of Consumer Affairs (DACO) under Act No. 5 of April 23, 1973.

sections.heading: 13. Severability clause
sections.body: If any provision of these Terms is found invalid, illegal, or unenforceable under applicable law, that provision shall be interpreted in the manner closest to its original intent that is valid; the remaining provisions shall remain in full force.

sections.heading: 14. Force majeure
sections.body:
Neither party will be liable for failure or delay in performing obligations under these Terms when caused by events beyond its reasonable control, including — without limitation — hurricanes, earthquakes, other natural disasters, fires, floods, epidemics or pandemics, governmental decrees, emergency orders, prolonged power or telecommunications outages, acts of terrorism, widespread labor disputes, or critical disruptions in the supply chain of USP/NF raw materials needed for the cohort's lab practice.

The affected party will notify the other with reasonable promptness, describe the nature of the event and its estimated impact, and make reasonable efforts to mitigate that impact. When a force majeure event prevents the in-person delivery of a cohort, SCCA may: (a) reschedule the cohort to reasonable later dates, or (b) offer a refund or transfer under the Refund Policy.

sections.heading: 15. Student indemnification
sections.body:
The student agrees to indemnify, defend, and hold harmless SCCA, its officers, instructors, employees, contractors, and affiliated providers (including Santa Cruz Pharma Care) from any claim, loss, liability, damage, cost, or expense (including reasonable attorneys' fees) arising from or related to: (a) the student's breach of laboratory safety standards or of the provisions in Section 5 (Student obligations); (b) the student's negligence, willful misconduct, or improper behavior during the cohort or on the premises; (c) misuse of instructional material or of SCCA's marks in violation of Section 6 (Intellectual property); or (d) any preparation, formulation, or procedure the student performs after the cohort in their own professional practice.

This indemnification obligation survives the termination or expiration of these Terms.

sections.heading: 16. Assignment, notices, and entire agreement
sections.body:
Assignment: the student may not assign, transfer, or delegate their rights or obligations under these Terms, except through the seat-transfer procedure established in the Refund Policy. SCCA may assign these Terms to a successor entity resulting from corporate reorganization, merger, acquisition, or substantial sale of assets, with prior reasonable notice to active students.

Notices: notices under these Terms will be considered validly served when sent by email: to SCCA, to info@sccompoundingacademy.com; to the student, to the email address provided at the time of enrollment. It is the student's responsibility to keep their contact information up to date during the cohort.

Entire agreement: these Terms, together with the Privacy Policy, the Refund Policy, and the cohort-specific data confirmed at enrollment, constitute the entire agreement between the student and SCCA regarding its subject matter, and supersede any prior agreement, promise, or understanding (oral or written) on that subject. No modification to these Terms will be valid unless made in writing and published on the Site in accordance with Section 11.

No waiver: the failure of either party to require strict compliance with a provision will not constitute a waiver of its right to require such compliance later.

sections.heading: 17. Contact
sections.body:
For questions or clarifications about these Terms:

Santa Cruz Compounding Academy, LLC
73 Santa Cruz Medical Building, Suite 201
Bayamón, Puerto Rico 00961
Email: info@sccompoundingacademy.com
Phone (primary): 787-408-5775
Phone (secondary): 787-798-4646

refund.title: Refund Policy
refund.intro: We know circumstances change. This policy defines when you can request a refund of your enrollment, how to transfer your seat if you cannot attend, and the applicable timelines. It forms an integral part of SCCA's Terms and Conditions.

sections.heading: 1. Full refund (30 or more calendar days before start)
sections.body:
If you request your refund thirty (30) or more calendar days before the first scheduled day of your cohort, we refund one hundred percent (100 %) of the amount paid, including any IVU tax actually collected, with no penalty.

The refund is processed by the same method you paid with (typically refund to the original card). SCCA issues the refund instruction to Stripe within five (5) business days following approval of the request; the actual time it appears on your statement depends on Stripe and the issuing bank, typically five (5) to ten (10) additional business days.

As an alternative to a monetary refund, you may transfer your seat at no penalty (see section 4).

sections.heading: 2. 50 % refund (between 14 and 30 calendar days before start)
sections.body:
If your request arrives fewer than thirty (30) calendar days but at least fourteen (14) calendar days before the first day of the cohort, we refund fifty percent (50 %) of the amount paid. The non-refunded portion covers materials, seat reservation, and logistical preparation costs already committed for your participation.

The partial refund is processed by the same original payment method, within the same timelines described in section 1.

As an alternative to the partial refund, you may transfer your seat at no penalty (see section 4); a transfer preserves one hundred percent (100 %) of your enrollment value.

sections.heading: 3. No monetary refund (fewer than 14 days before start, or after the cohort has begun)
sections.body:
Requests received fewer than fourteen (14) calendar days before the first day of the cohort, as well as those received once the cohort has begun, are not eligible for a monetary refund (zero percent, 0 %). Within that window, materials and operating costs are already committed.

You nevertheless keep the option to transfer your seat at no penalty (see section 4), which preserves the full value of your enrollment.

In duly documented exceptional cases — serious illness, family emergency, force-majeure situation — we will evaluate on a case-by-case basis the possibility of transferring your remaining seat to a future cohort of the same program. Such requests must be accompanied by supporting documentation (medical certificate, deployment order, etc.) and will be evaluated within ten (10) business days of receipt. The decision rests with SCCA's reasonable discretion.

sections.heading: 4. Seat transfer (alternative to a refund)
sections.body:
At any time before the first day of the cohort you may transfer your seat, with no penalty, instead of requesting a monetary refund. A transfer preserves one hundred percent (100 %) of the amount paid and is especially useful when your request falls within a partial-refund or no-refund window.

You have two options:

Transfer your seat to another eligible person. The designated person must (i) meet the same program eligibility requirements, (ii) expressly accept in writing the SCCA Terms and Conditions and Privacy Policy before starting the cohort, and (iii) confirm attendance at least twenty-four (24) hours before the first day. Enrollment data is updated to the new person's name and the certificate, where applicable, is issued in their name at the end of the cohort.

Transfer your seat to a future cohort of the same program within the next twelve (12) months. If the new cohort has a different price, the difference is adjusted (in your favor or billed separately).

To request a transfer, write to info@sccompoundingacademy.com with your name, original cohort date, and the chosen option (including the name and email of the designated person, if applicable).

sections.heading: 5. Cancellations by SCCA
sections.body:
If, for operational, regulatory, or force-majeure reasons, SCCA cancels a cohort before its start, we offer the enrolled student two options, at their choice:

Full refund of the amount paid, processed within no more than ten (10) business days from the cancellation notification.

Automatic priority transfer to the next scheduled cohort of the same program, at no additional cost and with no penalty.

If the cancellation occurs after the cohort has begun for reasons attributable to SCCA, the portion proportional to the undelivered material will be refunded, or completion of the program in a subsequent cohort will be offered.

sections.heading: 6. How to request a refund or transfer
sections.body:
All requests must be sent by email to info@sccompoundingacademy.com, including: (a) your full name, (b) the email you enrolled with, (c) course and cohort date, (d) type of request (refund, transfer to person, transfer to cohort), and (e) when applicable, supporting documentation.

We confirm receipt of your request within the following forty-eight (48) business hours and resolve within ten (10) business days thereafter.

sections.heading: 7. Cases not covered by this policy
sections.body:
This policy does not apply to, nor cover: (a) charges for optional services, additional materials, or equipment purchased separately; (b) enrollments in special events (one-off workshops, seminars) whose individual policy is published on its registration page; (c) partial absences of the student for own causes without prior notification; (d) issuing-bank charges (currency conversion, international transaction, intermediation), which are the sole responsibility of the student; nor (e) partial refunds for individual sessions of the cohort once the program has begun.

Chargebacks: if the student initiates a chargeback with their issuing bank without first contacting SCCA and exhausting the procedure under this policy, SCCA will submit the corresponding documentation to the issuing bank to dispute the chargeback and reserves the right to seek from the student the associated administrative costs, without prejudice to applicable legal actions.

In any scenario not expressly contemplated in this policy, SCCA will evaluate the situation in good faith prioritizing a fair resolution for both parties.

Student eligibility verification: the student matrícula is reviewed before payment is processed. Accordingly, if your matrícula cannot be verified or is rejected, no charge is made for the student rate and there is therefore no amount to refund; if you wish, you may enroll at the professional rate. The matrícula photo is deleted after the decision, whether approval or rejection.

sections.heading: 8. Contact
sections.body:
For questions about this policy or to initiate a request:

Santa Cruz Compounding Academy, LLC
73 Santa Cruz Medical Building, Suite 201
Bayamón, Puerto Rico 00961
Email: info@sccompoundingacademy.com
Phone (primary): 787-408-5775
Phone (secondary): 787-798-4646

hero.slogan: We educate to build wellness and health
hero.eyebrow: Professional certification · ACPE 0151 · 18 hrs · Bayamón, PR
hero.headline: Hands-on compounding training, inside a working laboratory.
hero.subheadline: 18-hour in-person course (3 days · 6 hours/day) in Bayamón for pharmacists and pharmacy technicians, with supervised practice and a curriculum aligned to USP 〈795〉 and USP 〈800〉. A program affiliated with Santa Cruz Pharma Care.
hero.imageAlt: Compounding bench with analytical balance, mortar, and USP/NF raw materials — Santa Cruz Pharma Care campus in Bayamón, Puerto Rico.

items.label: Affiliated program
items.body: Santa Cruz Compounding Academy grew out of Santa Cruz Pharma Care, a Bayamón pharmacy with over fifteen years of active practice in compounded preparations and patient-specific medications.

items.label: Supervised practice
items.body: Cohorts operate in the pharmacy's working laboratory. Every technique is performed the same day it's taught, with individual instructor review and a documented portfolio of preparations.

items.label: USP-aligned
items.body: Curriculum built chapter by chapter from the current USP 〈795〉 and 〈800〉. Regulatory updates are folded into the material between cohorts.

cursosGrid.heading: Course offered
cursosGrid.intro: An integrated 18-hour in-person program (3 days · 6 hrs/day) covering USP 〈795〉 and 〈800〉 across three consecutive modules. Theory and supervised practice on the same day, in the working lab at Santa Cruz Pharma Care.
cursosGrid.ceBody: {contactHours} contact hours · {ceus} CEUs · Knowledge-based, Level 1 · ACPE Provider {providerNumber} via {provider}
cursosGrid.priceNoteProfessional: Materials, lunch & ACPE CE certificate included
cursosGrid.priceNoteStudent: Portal access, quizzes & certificate of completion
studentHighlights: No ACPE CE credit — reserved for licensed professionals
otrosProfesionalesHighlights: Same USP 〈795〉 + 〈800〉 curriculum as the professional track
Certificate of completion — no ACPE CE credit
Small cohorts · materials and lunch included

items.title: Basic Non-Sterile Compounding — Pharmacists & Technicians
items.description: Integrated 18-hour in-person course for pharmacists and pharmacy technicians. Covers regulatory foundations (USP 〈795〉, 〈800〉, PR Pharmacy Act, DQSA), formulation, and clinical cases by specialty.

modules.title: Regulatory foundations and capsules
modules.summary: USP 〈795〉 and 〈800〉, scope of hazardous-drug handling, NIOSH, PPE, PR Pharmacy Act, DQSA and the 503B Outsourcing Facility category; BUD and packing-statistics math, with a hands-on slow-release capsule lab of 100 units.

modules.title: Suppositories, oral liquids, and dermatology
modules.summary: Suppository BUD and formulation, oral suspensions with dispensing math, topical conditions (acne, psoriasis, melasma, alopecia), pediatric cases, and pain-management preparations (analgesic creams and combinations).

modules.title: Topical hormones, veterinary, and regulation
modules.summary: BHRT (Bi-est, testosterone), the Topi-Click system, penetration enhancers, shortage and excipient-allergy exceptions, 503A vs 503B distinctions, and veterinary compounding.

items.title: Student Track — Nonsterile Compounding
items.description: An in-person program for pre-licensure students. Two didactic modules on the USP chapters that govern nonsterile compounding and hazardous-drug handling, taught at our Bayamón campus.

modules.title: USP 〈795〉 — Nonsterile Compounding
modules.summary: Scope of a compounded nonsterile preparation (CNSP), the designated person, facility standards, Beyond-Use Dates by water activity (aw), and distinguishing 〈795〉 from 〈797〉 and 〈800〉.

modules.title: USP 〈800〉 — Hazardous Drugs
modules.summary: NIOSH criteria, the HD list and Assessment of Risk, engineering controls (C-PEC, C-SEC), PPE including ASTM D6978 gloves, and spill and medical-surveillance procedures.

items.credentialNote: Certificate of completion — no ACPE continuing-education (CE) credit. CE credit is reserved for the professional track.

items.title: Other Professionals
items.description: The same 18-hour in-person course (USP 〈795〉 and 〈800〉) for other licensed non-pharmacist professionals. Same training and supervised practice; certificate of completion with no ACPE CE credit.

modules.title: Regulatory foundations and capsules
modules.summary: USP 〈795〉 and 〈800〉, NIOSH, PPE, PR Pharmacy Act, DQSA; BUD math and a capsule lab.

modules.title: Suppositories, oral liquids, and dermatology
modules.summary: Suppository formulation, oral suspensions, topical conditions, and pain-management preparations.

modules.title: Topical hormones, veterinary, and regulation
modules.summary: BHRT, Topi-Click, penetration enhancers, 503A/503B, and veterinary compounding.

items.credentialNote: Certificate of completion — no ACPE CE credit. CE credit is reserved for licensed pharmacists and pharmacy technicians.

modules.title: USP 〈795〉 — Nonsterile Compounding
modules.summary: Defining a compounded nonsterile preparation (CNSP) and its scope, the designated person's responsibilities, facility and component-handling standards, assigning Beyond-Use Dates by water activity (aw), and distinguishing 〈795〉 from 〈797〉 and 〈800〉.

modules.title: USP 〈800〉 — Handling Hazardous Drugs
modules.summary: NIOSH criteria for hazardous drugs, the HD list and Assessment of Risk, the three levels of engineering controls (C-PEC, C-SEC, supplemental), PPE selection including ASTM D6978 chemotherapy gloves, and receipt, spill, deactivation, and medical-surveillance procedures.

instructor.heading: Jorge L. Reyes Quiñones, RPh, FACA, FACVP
instructor.title: Chief Pharmacist and owner · Santa Cruz Pharma Care
instructor.name: Jorge L. Reyes Quiñones
instructor.roleLine: Chief Pharmacist & Owner — Santa Cruz Pharma Care, Bayamón

rows.label: Founder
rows.text: Founded Santa Cruz Pharma Care in Bayamón — a compounding pharmacy still in active daily practice.

rows.label: Educator
rows.text: Established Puerto Rico's first academic Pharmacy Technician program at Ana G. Méndez University.

rows.label: Specialist
rows.text: Advanced training at PCCA and the USP Convention; expert in hormonal therapy, veterinary, and pain-management formulations.

rows.label: Honored
rows.text: Preceptor of the Year at Creighton University, with an eponymous "Jorge L. Reyes Award" at Nova Southeastern University.

instructor.bio: Jorge L. Reyes Quiñones, RPh, FACA, FACVP — Chief Pharmacist and owner of Santa Cruz Pharma Care. Licensed pharmacist in Puerto Rico since 1980 with more than 45 years of professional practice, specialized compounding training through PCCA and the USP Convention, and recognitions including Preceptor of the Year 2025 (Creighton University) and the eponymous "Jorge L. Reyes Award" at Nova Southeastern University. Founder of Puerto Rico's first academic Pharmacy Technician program (Ana G. Méndez, 1985–88).
credentials: B.S.Ph. — School of Pharmacy, University of Puerto Rico, Río Piedras campus (1969–1972); PR License No. 2979, issued July 30, 1980
Comprehensive Compounding Course — Professional Compounding Centers of America (PCCA)
USP 〈795〉 + 〈797〉 certification — USP Convention Global Education and Training Program
Advanced clinical compounding — University of Florida College of Pharmacy (Hormone Restoration Therapy, Pain Management); Basic Veterinary Compounding — American College of Apothecaries
Emeterio Betances Award for contribution to non-sterile compounding education; eponymous "Jorge L. Reyes Award" at Nova Southeastern University for excellence in the compounding rotation
Preceptor of the Year 2025 — Creighton University School of Pharmacy; Clinical Affiliate Assistant Professor, Nova Southeastern University; Fellow of the American College of Veterinary Pharmacists
instructor.photoAlt: Jorge L. Reyes Quiñones, RPh, FACA, FACVP — professional portrait

timeline.text: Licensed in Puerto Rico. Founded Santa Cruz Pharma Care — still operating today after 45 years of compounding practice.
Founded Puerto Rico's first academic Pharmacy Technician program at Ana G. Méndez University (1985–88).
Advanced training at PCCA and the USP Convention. Specialized in hormone-restoration therapy, veterinary, and pain-management formulations.
Preceptor of the Year, Creighton University School of Pharmacy. Eponymous "Jorge L. Reyes Award" at Nova Southeastern University.

aprenderas.heading: Competencies you practice, document, and can have reviewed.
aprenderas.intro: The program trains six disciplines a pharmacist needs to prepare medications safely, document them rigorously, and stay compliant with current standards.

items.label: Preparation technique
items.body: Weighing, trituration, incorporation, and homogenization of non-sterile preparations — particle-size control, uniformity, and final-formulation verification.

items.label: Documentation and traceability
items.body: Master sheets, batch records, quality control, and labeling per USP 〈795〉 — inspection-ready for Pharmacy Board review.

items.label: Occupational safety under USP 〈800〉
items.body: Personal protective equipment, engineering controls, medical surveillance, and procedures that protect the team, the patient, and the environment.

items.label: BUD calculation
items.body: Beyond-Use Dating by preparation category, storage conditions, and dosage form.

items.label: Excipients and base handling
items.body: Selecting topical and oral bases, physicochemical compatibility, and the stability and preservation of the final preparation.

items.label: Compliance and audit readiness
items.body: Preparation for regulatory inspections, internal quality audits, and ongoing review of standard operating procedures.

paraQuienEs.heading: Designed for professionals who work with real preparations.
paraQuienEs.intro: We structure the courses for four distinct profiles. Each one enters with a defined professional goal and leaves with documented, immediately applicable competencies.

items.title: Licensed pharmacists
items.body: Practicing professionals who want to add compounding to their practice with technical judgment and regulatory grounding — or to deepen competencies they already exercise.

items.title: Pharmacy technicians
items.body: Technical staff assisting preparations who need structured training in procedures, safety, and quality documentation.

items.title: Owners and managers
items.body: Owners and administrators evaluating whether to open or expand a compounding service who need USP compliance from day one.

items.title: Students in transition
items.body: Advanced students and professionals changing fields who want a technical entry into compounding with real mentorship.

especialidades.heading: Practice areas the program covers.
especialidades.intro: The curriculum prepares graduates for the practice areas that Santa Cruz Pharma Care serves every day, applying the principles of USP 〈795〉 and 〈800〉 to each population and need.

items.label: Dermatological
items.body: Personalized creams for spots, wrinkles, and acne — formulating topical bases and handling active ingredients.

items.label: Hormonal
items.body: Progesterone, estrogens, and other bioidentical hormones tailored to each patient's specific needs.

items.label: Pediatric
items.body: Pediatric medications with kid-friendly flavors and weight-adjusted dosing.

items.label: Veterinary
items.body: Animal formulations adjusted by species, weight, and required presentation.

items.label: Topical anesthetics · BLT
items.body: BLT formulas (benzocaine, lidocaine, tetracaine) to minimize pain and maximize comfort in procedures.

items.label: General compounding
items.body: Personalization of medications for each patient's unique needs — alternative dosage forms and patient-specific excipients.

galeria.heading: Where each technique is trained.
galeria.intro: Three views of the campus's daily work: the controlled bench, the non-sterile preparation, and the safety cabinet for hazardous drugs.

items.caption: Compounding bench with analytical balance and classified raw materials.
Mortar and pestle in use during a non-sterile preparation.
Biological safety cabinet for hazardous drug handling under USP 〈800〉.

faqClean.heading: What pharmacists ask before enrolling.
faqClean.intro: If your question isn't here, write to us. We respond within 48 business hours.

ubicacion.heading: Campus in Bayamón, Puerto Rico.
ubicacion.intro: The academy shares a campus with Santa Cruz Pharma Care and operates inside its working laboratory. Minutes from PR-22 and PR-2.
ubicacion.addressLine: 73 Santa Cruz Medical Building, Suite 201, Bayamón, PR 00961
ubicacion.parkingNote: On-site parking available for cohort participants.
ubicacion.transitNote: Minutes from PR-22 and PR-2. Transit coordination available on request.
